content:
4 min read
Just now

--

Building a website used to mean paying for hosting before you’d even validated your idea. Not anymore. Between static site hosts, git-based deploy pipelines, and generous free tiers from major cloud providers, you can launch a fast, secure site today without spending a cent – as long as you know where to look.
Here are ten solid options, roughly ordered from easiest-to-start to most-flexible.
1. Github Pages
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Free static hosting tied directly to a GitHub repo. Push your HTML/CSS/JS (or a Jekyll site) to a repo, flip on Pages in settings, and you get a live URL in minutes. Great for portfolios, docs, and simple project sites. Custom domains are supported with a CNAME file.
2. Cloudflare Pages
Git-connected static hosting with a global CDN, free SSL, and generous build minutes. It handles frameworks like Next.js, Astro, and Hugo out of the box, and pairs nicely with Cloudflare Workers if you need serverless functions later. A strong pick if you want speed without touching server config.
3. Netlify
Press enter or click to view image in full size

One of the original JAMstack hosts. Connect a repo, get automatic builds and deploys on every push, free SSL, and a generous free tier with forms and basic serverless functions included. The dashboard is beginner-friendly.
4. Vercel
Built by the creators of Next.js, so it’s the smoothest option if you’re building a React/Next site. Free tier includes automatic deployments, preview URLs for every branch, and edge functions.
5. AWS Amplify Hosting
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Amazon’s git-based static hosting, part of the broader AWS free tier. Slightly more setup than Netlify or Vercel, but worth it if you’re already in the AWS ecosystem or want to grow into Lambda, DynamoDB, or Cognito later.
6. Firebase Hosting
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Google’s static hosting with a free “Spark” tier – free SSL, a global CDN, and simple CLI deploys. Pairs well if you want a free NoSQL database (Firestore) or auth bolted on later.
7. Render
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Render’s free static site hosting includes auto-deploys from Git and free SSL. It’s less known than Netlify/Vercel but has a clean UI and no surprise cold-start issues for static content.
8. Surge.sh
Press enter or click to view image in full size

About as bare-bones as it gets – install the CLI, run surge, and your site is live. No accounts dashboards, no git integration required. Perfect for quick prototypes or one-off landing pages.
9. Replit (Glitch)
Not just for hosting – these let you code and host in the browser with zero local setup. Good for quick demos, small tools, or teaching contexts, though they’re better suited to small apps than production sites.
10. InfinityFree
If you specifically need PHP and MySQL for free (WordPress, custom PHP apps), these old-school free hosts still exist. Expect ads on lower tiers, slower performance, and less reliability than the git-based options above – but they fill a niche the static hosts don’t.
